# Registry Visibility API reference

## Overview


## Concepts

1. **Registry Artifact Count**:
2. **Bulk Tagging**:
3. **Filtering by Tags and Environment Tags**:

## Registry Artifact Count

## Bulk tagging

Bulk tagging lets you add, remove, or replace project tags on multiple registry artifacts in a single request. A registry artifact is a module, provider, or component configuration.

HCP Terraform processes bulk tagging requests asynchronously, so you can affect a large number of artifacts in a single operation.

### Tagging permissions in artifact responses

The registry module, provider, and component configuration API responses include tagging permissions in the standard `permissions` attribute. Use these permissions to determine whether you can manage tags on an artifact before you submit a bulk action.

| Permission | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| `can-manage-registry-artifact-tags` | Indicates whether you can manage project tags on the artifact. |
| `can-manage-registry-artifact-version-tags` | Indicates whether you can manage environment tags on the artifact's versions. |

For example, a registry module response includes the following `permissions` attribute:

```json
{
  "id": "mod-Rp3q2xFoj6Dh8D6Z",
  "type": "registry-modules",
  "attributes": {
    "name": "partner-boundary-worker",
    "namespace": "HashiCorp-Partner-Lab",
    "permissions": {
      "can-delete": true,
      "can-resync": true,
      "can-retry": true,
      "can-manage-registry-artifact-tags": true,
      "can-manage-registry-artifact-version-tags": true
    }
  }
}
```

### Submit a bulk tagging action

Use the following endpoints to submit a bulk tagging job. Each endpoint performs a different action on the target artifacts.

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| `POST` | `/api/v2/tag-bindings/bulk-actions/add` | Adds the specified tags to the targets. When a target already contains a tag, HCP Terraform ignores that tag. |
| `POST` | `/api/v2/tag-bindings/bulk-actions/remove` | Removes the specified tags from the targets. When a target does not contain a tag, HCP Terraform ignores that tag. |
| `POST` | `/api/v2/tag-bindings/bulk-actions/set` | Replaces all tags on the targets with the specified tags. |

The request payload contains a list of target artifacts and a list of tag bindings to apply to each target. Each request must meet the following requirements:

- All targets must be the same resource type. You cannot modify a registry module and a registry provider in the same request.
- All targets must reside in the same organization, even when you have access to more than one organization.
- You can modify up to 100 artifacts and up to 30 tags per request.

When you do not have permission to modify one of the targets, HCP Terraform modifies none of the targets. Either all artifacts change or none do.

| Status | Response | Reason |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| [202](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/202) | [JSON API document](/terraform/cloud-docs/api-docs#json-api-documents) | Successfully scheduled the bulk tagging job. |
| [400](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/400) | [JSON API error object](http://jsonapi.org/format/#error-objects) | Malformed request body, for example a missing relationship, an incorrect data type, or a request that does not meet the resource type and organization requirements. |
| [404](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/404) | [JSON API error object](http://jsonapi.org/format/#error-objects) | You are not authorized to modify one or more targets, or a target does not exist. |

#### Sample payload

The following payload adds two tags to two registry modules. The `bar` tag includes a value, and the `foo` tag is a key with no value.

```json
{
  "data": {
    "type": "bulk-tagging-actions",
    "relationships": {
      "targets": {
        "data": [
          {
            "id": "mod-WKZBbsFkTExqosLi",
            "type": "registry-modules"
          },
          {
            "id": "mod-pjcbMFbKWEFNW5b6",
            "type": "registry-modules"
          }
        ]
      },
      "tag-bindings": {
        "data": [
          {
            "type": "tag-bindings",
            "attributes": { "key": "foo" }
          },
          {
            "type": "tag-bindings",
            "attributes": { "key": "bar", "value": "baz" }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

#### Sample request

```shell-session
curl \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  --header "Content-Type: application/vnd.api+json" \
  --request POST \
  --data @payload.json \
  https://app.terraform.io/api/v2/tag-bindings/bulk-actions/add
```

#### Sample response

HCP Terraform schedules the job and returns a job ID, a `pending` status, and a polling URL in the `self` link.

```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": "bulktagging-WKZBbsFkTExqosLi",
    "type": "tag-binding-bulk-actions",
    "attributes": {
      "status": "pending"
    },
    "links": {
      "self": "/api/v2/tag-bindings/bulk-actions/bulktagging-WKZBbsFkTExqosLi"
    }
  }
}
```

#### Error response

When a request is malformed, HCP Terraform returns an HTTP error code with a JSON API error object for each failure. HCP Terraform does not modify any artifacts when it returns an error response.

```json
{
  "errors": [
    {
      "status": "400",
      "title": "bad request",
      "detail": "Missing targets relationship",
      "source": {
        "pointer": "/data/relationships"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```
